name: Manali
description: Manali is a high-altitude Himalayan resort town nestled in the Kullu Valley of Himachal Pradesh at an elevation of 2,050 metres. Framed by soaring snow-capped peaks, lush deodar forests, and the rushing Beas River, it is one of India's most beloved mountain destinations. The town uniquely blends the spiritual calm of Old Manali's apple orchards and ancient temples with a thriving adventure tourism scene offering skiing, paragliding, trekking, and river rafting.
history: Manali derives its name from "Manu-Alaya" (abode of Manu), the mythical Hindu sage who is believed to have meditated here after the great deluge. The region has been a significant trade route between India and Central Asia via the Rohtang Pass for centuries. The British established it as a rest-and-recreation hill station in the colonial era.
bestSeason: October to June (Snow: Dec–Mar, Pleasant: Apr–Jun & Sep–Oct)
nearestAirport: Bhuntar Airport (Kullu–Manali Airport), 50 km — code KUU
nearestRailwayStation: Joginder Nagar Railway Station, 165 km (narrow gauge)
nearestBusStand: Manali HRTC Bus Stand, Mall Road

topAttractions.name: Rohtang Pass
topAttractions.description: A breathtaking mountain pass at 3,978 m on the Kullu–Lahaul border, offering spectacular glacier views, snow even in summer, and access to Lahaul & Spiti. Requires online permit (₹500 per vehicle). Best visited May–June and Sep–Oct.

topAttractions.name: Solang Valley (Snow Point)
topAttractions.description: A 14 km long valley famous for skiing in winter and paragliding, zorbing, and horse-riding in summer. Cable car ride (₹800 return) offers panoramic views of surrounding peaks.

topAttractions.name: Hadimba Devi Temple
topAttractions.description: A 16th-century cave shrine dedicated to Hadimba, wife of Bhima of the Mahabharata. Built in 1553, it features a four-tiered pagoda roof of wooden carvings — a unique example of Himachali architecture. Open 8 AM–6 PM, free entry.

topAttractions.name: Jogini Waterfall
topAttractions.description: A stunning 160-ft multi-tiered waterfall accessible via a scenic 2 km trek from Vashisht village. The trail passes through terraced fields and pine forests. Best in monsoon (July–Aug) and post-monsoon.

topAttractions.name: Vashisht Hot Springs
topAttractions.description: Natural sulfur hot springs near the ancient Vashisht temple complex with separate bathing areas for men and women. The mineral-rich waters are said to have therapeutic properties. Free entry; temple complex is historically significant.

topAttractions.name: Naggar Castle
topAttractions.description: A 15th-century stone-and-wood castle-hotel overlooking the Kullu Valley. Once the capital of Kullu, now partly a heritage hotel and partly a museum. Nicholas Roerich Art Gallery is adjacent. Offers stunning valley views.

topAttractions.name: Beas Kund Trek
topAttractions.description: A 3-day trek (22 km round trip) to the glacial source of the Beas River at 3,700 m. A perfect beginner trek offering views of Hanuman Tibba and Friendship Peak. Best July–September.
topAttractions.location: Starts from Dhundi, 15 km from Manali

localFood: Siddu (steamed wheat bread stuffed with poppy seeds or walnuts)
Babru (black gram flour flatbread served with tamarind chutney)
Trout Fish (grilled or fried, from the Beas River)
shopping: Kullu shawls and pattus at Bhuttico (₹800–8,000)
Woolen socks and caps at Mall Road shops
Local honey and jams at Old Manali
culture: Manali is a cultural mosaic of Kullu's Hindu traditions and Tibetan Buddhist influences. The Dussehra of Kullu Valley (October) is one of India's largest fairs, when the deity of Raghunath is paraded through the streets. Old Manali retains its hippie-trail charm from the 1970s when it was a stop on the "hippie trail" to Kathmandu.
festivals: Kullu Dussehra (October — 7-day festival, one of the most vibrant in India)
hotels: The Himalayan Hotel & Spa (4-star, ₹6,000–12,000/night, Mall Road)
Span Resort & Spa (5-star, ₹12,000–25,000, 15 km from town)
Banjara Camp & Retreat (Luxury tents, ₹8,000+, Kullu Valley)
restaurants: Johnson Bar & Restaurant (Continental + Indian, ₹600–1,500, near Circuit House)
The Lazy Dog Lounge (Multi-cuisine, ₹500–1,200, Old Manali)
travelTips: Book Rohtang Pass permit (max 1,200 vehicles/day) online at hptdc.in at least 24 hrs in advance.
Carry cash — ATMs can run dry in peak season. SBI, PNB ATMs near Mall Road.
Avoid July–August monsoon due to landslides on Manali–Rohtang road.
Altitude acclimatization: rest on day 1, avoid alcohol, stay hydrated.
BSNL has best network coverage in remote areas. Airtel works in town.
Taxis are metered — negotiate from HRTC taxi stand for best rates.
Rohtang Pass closes Nov–April. Access only via special tunnels.
Atal Tunnel (Rohtang) is now open year-round for Lahaul access.
packingTips: Heavy winter jacket and thermal inners (even in May)
Sunscreen SPF 50+ and UV sunglasses (high UV at altitude)
weather: Winter (Dec–Feb): −10°C to 5°C, heavy snowfall, roads often blocked. Spring (Mar–Apr): 5–15°C, snow begins melting, good for skiing. Summer (May–Jun): 15–25°C, perfect weather, peak tourist season. Monsoon (Jul–Aug): 10–20°C, heavy rain and landslides — not recommended. Autumn (Sep–Oct): 10–20°C, post-monsoon clarity, excellent views.
transport: HRTC Volvo buses from Delhi ISBT Kashmiri Gate daily (₹1,200–1,800, 14 hrs). Private taxis from Delhi ~₹8,000–12,000. Nearest airport at Bhuntar (Kullu), 50 km. From Bhuntar, taxi to Manali costs ₹1,200–1,500. Within Manali: autos, e-rickshaws, and cycle rentals on Mall Road.

activities.description: After a night bus from Delhi, freshen up at your hotel. Take it easy to acclimatize.
Try local thali with rajma-chawal, himachali dal and stuffed paratha.
16th-century pagoda shrine in Dhungri Forest. Spend 45 minutes.
Walk through the apple orchard trails, cross the Manalsu Bridge to Old Manali cafes.
Browse Tibetan market stalls, try momos and kachori from street vendors.
Famous for continental food and live music. Grilled trout recommended.
Start early — road gets congested by 9 AM. Carry your e-permit. Shared taxi from Mall Road ₹350 each.
Snow play, photography, stunning views of Lahaul valley. Hire snow gear on site.

activities.activity: Lunch at Dhaba on Rohtang road
activities.description: Hot Maggi noodles and chai at roadside dhabas — essential Rohtang experience.
Stop on the way back. Try zorbing (₹300) or cable car (₹800 return). Allow 2 hours.
Soak tired muscles in the natural sulfur baths. Separate men/women pools.

activities.activity: Dinner at The Lazy Dog Lounge
activities.description: Try the himachali trout and chocolate brownie.
Tandem paragliding with trained pilots. 1,500 m flight above the valley. Advance booking recommended.
Buy Kullu shawls at Bhuttico co-operative (fixed price, best quality), Tibetan artifacts.
Quick lunch at Chopsticks Restaurant, then board the HRTC Volvo bus back to Delhi.
Check in, short rest. Walk to Hadimba Temple (15 min). Evening at Mall Road.
Welcome dinner with local cuisine and live music.
Early departure for maximum time at the pass. Snow activities, photography.
Cable car and adventure sports on the way back.
Drive 21 km to the historic castle. Visit the Nicholas Roerich Art Gallery adjacent.
White-water rafting on Beas River near Pirdi (14 km rapids, Grade II–III). ₹600–1,200 per person.
Therapeutic soak after the rafting adventure.
Drive to Jobra (10 km), trek through alpine meadows. 8 km round trip, moderate difficulty.
Tandem flight with panoramic Himalayan views.
Arrange a bonfire at your camp or hotel garden. Traditional Himachali food.
Last morning for souvenir shopping. Kullu shawls, honey jams, carved wooden items.
Lunch at local dhaba, then begin the bus/cab journey back.
Check in to a family-friendly resort with lawn area.
Easy stroll through the forest temple and national park — great for kids.
Kids love the Tibetan street food and ice cream shops.
Kids can build snowmen and ride snow scooters. Very safe and fun.
Gentle slopes for zorbing, Solang valley pony rides for children.
Walk through apple orchards — kids love picking seasonal apples (May–Sep).
Book a cab for full family. Dress in rented snow gear and enjoy.
Visit shawl weaving cooperative at Mohal — educational for kids.
Good multi-cuisine menu, kid-friendly.
Short hike (2 km) to the waterfall — very manageable for families with older kids.
Kullu shawls, woolen caps, snow globe souvenirs for kids.
Drive to Bhuntar Airport or Chandigarh.
Acclimatize, rent trekking gear at Manali market.
Walk along the river, identify rafting put-in points.
14 km stretch from Pirdi to Jhiri. Rapids: Blackhole, Cross Fire. Thrilling class III–IV.
Extended tandem flight with experienced pilot. Aerial photography included.
Drive to Jobra. Begin trek to Chika campsite (2 km, 3 hrs). Setting up in alpine meadows.
Campfire, stargazing, Himalayan views from your tent.
Trek 8 km to the pass. Stunning views of Spiti Valley on the other side. Return to Shea Goru.
Camp by the glacial stream. Full Himalayan wilderness immersion.
Descend via Chika back to Jobra. Drive to Manali.
Soak tired muscles in the natural thermal springs.
Celebratory dinner at Johnson Bar Restaurant.
